data.description:
## What

**Retire the band-aids** now that the self-healing ref-lock (C3) is authoritative, and prove no
coordination state remains in the tickets tree.

- Remove `.reconciler-*` from the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` written by
  `src/rebar/_commands/init.py:55-56` (leave `.bridge_state/*` untouched — that is WS-1 / binding
  data, out of scope), and update `tests/unit/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py` accordingly.
- Remove the `b859-8fa1` lock-retry band-aid loop in
  `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py:73-89, 263-291` (superseded by
  lease-based self-heal).
- One-time migration: on reconciler startup (or via `bridge_fsck`/init), clean up any
  `.reconciler-pass-lock`/`.reconciler-phase-gate` left committed on the tickets branch from the
  old scheme.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.reconciler-*` no longer appears in the tickets-branch `.gitattributes` (`_commands/init.py`);
      `.bridge_state/*` `merge=ours` is left in place.
- [ ] `tests/unit/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py` updated to assert `.reconciler-*` is absent
      and `.bridge_state/*` still present.
- [ ] The `b859` retry loop (`_advisory_lock.py:73-89, 263-291`) is deleted; contention is handled
      by the ref-lock CAS/lease path instead.
- [ ] Migration removes any pre-existing committed `.reconciler-*` files; regression test asserts
      no `.reconciler-*` path is ever committed to the tickets branch.
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green.
data.title: Retire merge=ours .gitattributes + b859 lock-retry band-aid; migrate committed locks
